title: Dell updates Alienware gaming laptops with Intel s Arrow Lake-HX Refresh CPUs 16-inch models receive OLED display and GPU upgrades
summary: Dell updates Alienware 16-inch gaming laptops with Intel Arrow Lake-HX Refresh CPUs, featuring OLED displays and GPU upgrades.
insight: Faster processors enhance gaming performance and user experience.

title: Micron enters high-volume production of HBM4 for Nvidia Vera Rubin - 2.3x bandwidth improvement and 20% boost in power efficiency
summary: Micron has entered high-volume production of HBM4 for Nvidia Vera Rubin, offering a 2.3x bandwidth improvement and 20% power efficiency boost.
insight: High-performance computing gains significant speed and efficiency boost.

title: Jensen Huang expects Nvidia to sell $1 trillion of AI hardware through 2027 AI buildout intensifies as Agentic AI takes hold
summary: Nvidia co-founder Jensen Huang predicts the company will earn $1 trillion from AI hardware sales by 2027, driven by the increasing adoption of artificial intelligence.
insight: AI's rapid growth and adoption drive trillion-dollar hardware sales projections

title: Nvidia announces Vera Rubin Space Module up to 25x the AI compute of H100 for orbital data centers
summary: Nvidia has announced the Vera Rubin Space Module, offering up to 25x the AI compute of H100. This platform is designed for orbital data centers, enhancing space-based AI capabilities. Six commercial space companies have already deployed the platform.
insight: Accelerating space-based AI capabilities with unprecedented computational power

title: Nvidia's Nemotron coalition brings eight AI labs together to build open frontier models
summary: Nvidia's Nemotron coalition brings together eight AI labs to build open frontier models, fostering innovation and global participation in the AI revolution.
insight: Open models accelerate AI innovation and global collaboration

title: Intel Xeon 6 selected as host CPU for Nvidia DGX Rubin NVL8 systems Intel wins a contract as Nvidia enters data center processor market with Vera CPUs
summary: Intel's Xeon 6 processor will be the host CPU in Nvidia's DGX Rubin NVL8 systems. This partnership marks a significant collaboration between the two tech giants. Intel wins a contract as Nvidia enters the data center processor market with Vera CPUs.
insight: Nvidia's entry into data center processors challenges AMD and Intel dominance

title: Nvidia Groq 3 LPU and Groq LPX racks join Rubin platform at GTC SRAM-packed accelerator boosts 'every layer of the AI model on every token'
summary: Nvidia unveiled the Groq 3 LPU and Groq LPX rack at GTC 2026, enhancing the Vera Rubin platform with SRAM-packed accelerators for low-latency AI model interactions.
insight: Boosting memory bandwidth for large AI models and complex contexts.

title: Nvidia unveils details of new 88-core Vera CPUs positioned to compete with AMD and Intel new Vera CPU rack features 256 liquid-cooled chips that deliver up to a 6X gain in CPU throughput
summary: Nvidia unveiled its Vera data center CPUs with 88 cores, offering 50% performance gains over standard CPUs. The new architecture includes a 1.5X increase in IPC from its Olympus cores. The Vera CPU Rack features 256 liquid-cooled CPUs for CPU-centric workloads.
insight: Nvidia's Vera CPUs challenge AMD and Intel's CPU dominance strongly now.

title: Nvidia debuts DLSS 5 for increased visual fidelity in games AI-infused tech transforms pixels with photorealistic lighting and materials
summary: Nvidia introduces DLSS 5, enhancing visual fidelity in games with AI-driven technology.
insight: Transforming pixels with photorealistic lighting and materials boosts gaming realism greatly.

title: Meta's new MTIA lineup joins hyperscalers' unified push for dedicated inferencing chips companies diversify AI chips in effort to diversify from sole reliance on Nvidia
summary: Meta joins hyperscalers in diversifying AI chips, reducing reliance on Nvidia. The company's new MTIA lineup targets specific workloads, citing cost-effectiveness for inference tasks. This move aims to optimize AI performance and efficiency.
insight: Diversifying AI chips reduces reliance on a single dominant supplier.

title: Microsoft and Samsung scramble to fix a major C: drive lockout bug on Galaxy devices faulty Galaxy Connect app leaves users with 'limited' recovery options following recent Windows 11 update
summary: A Samsung app bug on Windows 11 causes C: drive lockout after a recent security update. The faulty Galaxy Connect app has left users with limited recovery options. Microsoft and Samsung are working to fix the issue.
insight: Critical bug compromises user data access on Galaxy devices

title: Nvidia GTC 2026 keynote live blog Vera Rubin GPUs and CPUs, DLSS 5, and the 'future of technology'
summary: Nvidia's GTC 2026 keynote unveiled the Vera Rubin GPUs and CPUs, promising significant advancements in AI and computing. The event also showcased the latest iteration of DLSS, a key technology for enhanced gaming and graphics performance. CEO Jensen Huang emphasized the 'future of technology' throughout the presentation.
insight: Nvidia's Vera Rubin technology fuels AI and computing breakthroughs

title: Creative updates its Sound Blaster PCIe sound card line after 5 years new $79.99 Audigy FX Pro 7.1 pitched as clear upgrade over standard onboard audio
summary: Creative has released an updated Sound Blaster Audigy FX Pro 7.1 sound card, offering improved audio quality and features for PC desktop DIYers. This newest addition to the line is pitched as a clear upgrade over standard onboard audio. The Audigy FX Pro 7.1 is available at a price point of $79.99.
insight: Enhanced audio quality for PC desktop DIYers at an affordable price point.

title: Intel's roadmaps examined 14A, Nova Lake, Diamond Rapids & AI accelerator push
summary: Intel's CPU roadmap is ambitious and unprecedented in its complexity, requiring simultaneous success in manufacturing and product launches. The company is pushing to adopt new processes like 14A, Nova Lake, and Diamond Rapids while also developing AI accelerators. This multi-pronged approach presents significant challenges.
insight: Simultaneous manufacturing and product success is Intel's unprecedented challenge.

title: First credible leak of an AMD Zen 6 processor pops up on Geekbench ten-core CPU seems to have 32MB of L3 cache
summary: AMD's Zen 6 processor has been leaked on Geekbench with 10 cores and 32MB of L3 cache, indicating early development progress.
insight: AMD's Zen 6 development is underway, confirming future CPU improvements

title: GTC 2026
summary: GTC 2026 showcases Nvidia's advancements in AI technology and future innovations.
insight: Accelerating AI adoption transforms industries and the global digital landscape.

title: STMicro to deploy humanoid robots to its legacy fabs in Europe over 100 humanoid robots to be used for routine and physically demanding tasks in fight for efficiency
summary: STMicroelectronics plans to deploy 100 humanoid robots in European fabs to boost efficiency, tackling physically demanding tasks and improving productivity in the face of cheap Chinese chip imports.
insight: Robots combat economic threat from cheap Chinese chip imports in Europe.

title: Chinese GPU vendor Zephyr has cancelled its single-fan RTX 4070 Ti Super due to VRAM price hikes memory shortage is forcing a pivot to an SFF RTX 4070 Super instead
summary: Zephyr, a Chinese GPU vendor, cancels its RTX 4070 Ti Super due to VRAM shortages. The company has shifted its focus to develop an SFF RTX 4070 Super instead. This pivot is likely a response to the ongoing memory crisis in the industry.
insight: Memory shortages force Chinese GPU vendors to adapt and pivot models

title: Flabbergasted GPU repair wizard highlights dangers of liquid metal after leak kills entire RTX 5070 Ti user-applied TIM spread to every crevice of the PCB, physically cracking and shorting out the core
summary: A user-applied liquid metal TIM leak caused catastrophic damage to an RTX 5070 Ti, shorting out multiple components and killing the core. The damage was so extensive that replacement of key ICs would be necessary. This incident highlights the risks of using liquid metal TIM.
insight: Liquid metal TIM can cause irreversible damage to electronics components.

title: MSI plans 30% gaming product price hike as memory and GPU shortages bite 'This year is the most severe year since the company was founded'
summary: MSI plans a 30% price hike for its gaming products due to memory and GPU shortages. The company considers this year the most severe in its history. MSI is not alone in facing these challenges.
insight: Component shortages drive up gaming hardware prices significantly this year.

title: Mini review of the ThinkNode M6 outdoor solar power for Meshtastic
summary: The ThinkNode M6 outdoor solar power for Meshtastic is reviewed for its range and functionality in a suburban environment. The M6 is used as a router between two other ThinkNode nodes, M1 and M2. The review aims to assess the device's performance and potential in a real-world setting.
insight: Increased range and functionality for Meshtastic network in suburban areas.

title: Thin Raspberry Pi CM5 fanless mini PC offers HDMI video output, 2.5GbE + GbE, dual USB, M.2 Key-M socket
summary: The Waveshare Gigabit / 2.5G Dual Ethernet Mini-Computer (B) is a thin, fanless mini PC based on the Raspberry Pi CM5, offering a 4K HDMI video output, dual Ethernet ports, USB ports, and an M.2 Key-M socket for NVMe SSD or AI accelerator. It features a compact design with accessible GPIO headers and MIPI connectors. The mini PC supports a range of SoMs, including Raspberry Pi CM5 and CM5 Lite.
insight: Raspberry Pi's CM5 fanless mini PC offers high-speed networking and compact design.

title: Rabbit-Labs Flipper Zero ESP32-C5 multi-board features CC1101, GPS, and dual-band Wi-Fi 6
summary: The Flipper Zero ESP32-C5 multi-board features dual-band Wi-Fi 6 and a GPS module, alongside a sub-GHz transceiver, making it a compact alternative to bulkier add-ons.
insight: Compact design for versatile wireless capabilities and GPS.

title: Industrial Wi-Fi 7 access point with redundant 12V 48V DC inputs targets autonomous systems
summary: Advantech's EKI-6333BE-4GD is an industrial-grade Wi-Fi 7 access point designed for autonomous systems, offering up to 5.8 Gbps throughput, redundant DC inputs, and IEC 62443 compliance.
insight: Industrial-grade Wi-Fi 7 access point for autonomous systems boosts reliability

title: EDGE AI FOUNDATION expands partnership with Sensors Converge
summary: EDGE AI FOUNDATION expands its partnership with Sensors Converge by featuring a larger pavilion at the event. This pavilion will host over seven companies from May 5-7. The event aims to showcase innovative sensor technologies.
insight: Advancing AI capabilities through collaborative sensor technology innovation.

title: Announcing more Rising Stars 2026 (Fifth Group)
summary: The fifth group of Rising Stars 2026 honors innovators advancing technology with creativity, expertise, and impact.
insight: Accelerating tech innovation drives global progress and economic growth.

title: Meet the 102-year-old teaching seniors how to use smartphones and Windows
summary: A 102-year-old man leads a volunteer group teaching seniors digital literacy, covering topics like Windows 11 and online safety.
insight: Intergenerational transfer of knowledge bridges age gaps in digital skills.

title: Nvidia DLSS 5 first look: generative AI lighting radically transforms game visuals
summary: Nvidia's DLSS 5 transforms game visuals with generative AI lighting, surpassing upscaling and ray tracing.
insight: DLSS 5 redefines game visuals with AI-powered lighting innovation.

title: Canva Affinity adds Light UI, Convert to Curves, and Live Tone Blend
summary: Affinity's latest update introduces a new Light UI, Convert to Curves feature, and Live Tone Blend Groups, enhancing user experience and productivity.
insight: Streamlines design workflow with enhanced UI and powerful features.

title: Creative's Sound Blaster Audigy FX Pro brings discrete audio back from the grave
summary: Creative's new Sound Blaster Audigy FX Pro sound card offers a fresh take on discrete audio, aiming to upgrade the audio experience for a wider range of customers. This model includes an additional layer of functionality compared to previous versions. It is designed to cater to enthusiasts and hardware enthusiasts.
insight: Reviving discrete sound cards for modern users with enhanced features.

title: Microsoft is backing away from putting Copilot everywhere in Windows 11
summary: Microsoft has scrapped AI-powered Copilot Suggestions in Windows 11 due to backlash. The feature was initially previewed in 2024 but never rolled out. This decision comes after criticism of the Windows Recall tool.
insight: Microsoft prioritizes user trust over AI-driven feature adoption.

title: Doom: The Dark Ages is the first Denuvo DRM game of 2025 to be cracked
summary: Doom: The Dark Ages has been cracked by 'Voices38', making it Denuvo-free. The game is a significant download at 81 GB, requiring powerful hardware. This marks the first Denuvo DRM game of 2025 to be cracked.
insight: Denuvo DRM's effectiveness questioned as first game of 2025 is cracked.

title: Apple launches AirPods Max 2 with stronger ANC, lossless audio, and live translation
summary: Apple's AirPods Max 2 features stronger ANC, lossless audio, and live translation. Pre-orders open March 25 in over 30 countries, with general availability in early April. The design remains similar to the original AirPods Max.
insight: Enhanced audio capabilities boost AirPods Max 2's overall user experience.

title: GIMP 3.2 arrives with performance improvements and workflow refinements
summary: GIMP 3.2 has been updated with performance improvements and new features like non-destructive layers, an upgraded brush tool, and expanded file format support.
insight: Enhances creative workflow and versatility of digital image editing software.

title: Meta is pulling the plug on Instagram's encrypted messages
summary: Meta is discontinuing end-to-end encryption on Instagram's direct messages. Unlike WhatsApp and Facebook Messenger, Instagram's E2E encryption was manual and limited. This move may compromise user privacy.
insight: User privacy faces significant risks with manual E2E encryption.

title: Digg relaunch fails after two months as bots and AI agents flood the site
summary: Digg's relaunched beta version has been shut down due to issues with bots and AI agents flooding the site, leading to significant downsizing of the development team.
insight: Social media platforms struggle with AI-driven spam and bot attacks.

title: Microsoft says Samsung Galaxy app bug, not Windows update, locked some users out of C: drive
summary: Microsoft attributes C: drive lockout issue to Samsung app bug, not Windows update.
insight: Samsung app bug affected Windows users, highlighting ecosystem interdependence issues.

title: Samsung wins in court after German judge says some TCL "QLED" TVs aren't really QLED
summary: A German court ruled in favor of Samsung, stating TCL's 'QLED' TVs don't meet QLED standards, misleading consumers about color reproduction.
insight: QLED authenticity matters for consumer trust in display technology claims.

title: Google quietly pulls AI "health tool" that summarized Reddit advice
summary: Google quietly removed its AI-powered 'What People Suggest' tool, which summarized advice from online forums.
insight: Risks of AI-powered advice aggregation and misinformation dissemination highlighted.

title: Hacker unveils exploit that cracks the "unbreakable" Xbox One at the silicon level
summary: A hacker has successfully breached the Xbox One's hardware-level defenses, marking a milestone in console hacking. The breach targets the boot ROM on the Xbox, operating below the software stack. This achievement recalls the famous Reset Glitch Hack that compromised the Xbox 360 years earlier.
insight: Exploiting hardware vulnerabilities exposes critical security weaknesses in gaming consoles.

title: Grandmother spent six months in jail after AI facial recognition misidentified her
summary: A grandmother was mistakenly arrested and jailed for six months after an AI facial recognition error.
insight: AI facial recognition mistakes can have devastating consequences and long-term impact.

title: Nvidia's next AI chip may move beyond the all-purpose GPU
summary: Nvidia is considering a departure from its all-purpose GPU approach for AI computing, marking a shift in philosophy. This change would allow for more specialized chips, potentially leading to improved performance and efficiency. The move is in response to growing demand for faster AI computing.
insight: Specialized AI chips could lead to significant performance gains
